title: ascalea nutans
intro: Ascalea nutans, also known as the White Aspen or Quaking Aspen, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to North America, characterized by its unique white or greenish-white flowers and quivering leaves. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are derived from its bark, leaves, and roots. The health benefits of Ascalea nutans include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and soothing digestive problems, while its herbal preparations include tea, tinctures, and salves. Additionally, the plant's bark has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including wounds, skin conditions, and coughs.
